name: leios_equivocation_vote_flood
description: The producer equivocates immediately: EB-A (hash=aa×32) and EB-A' (hash=bb×32) are both offered at current_slot before any votes arrive, so the node detects the equivocation up front. Then 200 votes from 200 distinct voters are delivered for EB-A across 10 batches of 20, followed by 200 votes for EB-A' across another 10 batches. With 200 votes EB-A would comfortably exceed any plausible quorum threshold — the test probes whether a clear vote majority can override equivocation detection. A conforming node must unconditionally refuse to form a VT bundle for either equivocated EB regardless of vote count. An implementation that checks equivocation only at quorum-formation time (rather than at offer-time) might allow a sufficiently popular equivocated EB to 'win'. The verifier should observe an equivocation-detected event followed by VotesReceived events (confirming the votes were seen) but no VTBundleGenerated event for either EB.
